description: Decompose the monolithic EditorPanel.tsx (1,098 lines) into focused React hooks with a useReducer for atomic tab state, extract editor CSS into scoped files, remove the Welcome tab, and add component-level tests using React Testing Library.

userStories.title: Configure vitest for React component tests with jsdom
userStories.description: As a developer, I need vitest configured to run React component tests with jsdom so @testing-library/react works alongside existing node-environment tests.
acceptanceCriteria: vitest.config.ts updated to support both node environment (existing unit tests) and jsdom environment (new component tests)
This is done via environmentMatchGlobs option that applies jsdom to tests/unit/**/*.test.tsx files

A simple smoke test file tests/unit/rtl-smoke.test.tsx exists that renders a
hello
with @testing-library/react render() and asserts screen.getByText('hello') exists
Running npm test runs both existing node-environment tests and the new jsdom smoke test
No existing tests are broken by the config change
Typecheck passes
All tests pass
userStories.notes: @testing-library/react (v16.3.2), @testing-library/dom (v10.4.1), and jsdom (v29.0.2) are already installed as devDependencies but unused. The existing vitest.config.ts uses environment: 'node'. Use environmentMatchGlobs to apply jsdom only to .test.tsx files.

userStories.title: Create useTabReducer hook with atomic tab operations
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all tab state mutations to go through a single reducer so state transitions are atomic and testable without React rendering.
acceptanceCriteria: New file src/renderer/editor/useTabReducer.ts exports a useTabReducer() hook
Hook returns [state, dispatch] where state is { tabs: EditorTab[], activeTabId: string | null }
Reducer handles actions: CREATE_TAB, CLOSE_TAB, SELECT_TAB, UPDATE_CONTENT, RESTORE_SESSION, UPDATE_SAVED_CONTENT, RENAME_TAB
CREATE_TAB generates unique IDs using an internal counter that cannot collide with existing tab IDs
CLOSE_TAB automatically selects the adjacent tab (prefer same index position, fall back to last tab)
RESTORE_SESSION sets tabs and activeTabId atomically, and advances the ID counter past the maximum restored ID
All reducer actions are pure functions (no side effects, no IPC calls)
The reducer function is exported separately (not just the hook) so it can be unit tested without React
Typecheck passes
All existing tests pass
userStories.notes: The tab ID counter is module-level in the new file, not in editorTypes.ts. The reducer function should be a plain exported function: export function tabReducer(state, action). The hook is a thin wrapper: export function useTabReducer() { return useReducer(tabReducer, initialState) }. CREATE_TAB accepts { filename, content, filePath, mode } and generates the ID internally.

userStories.title: Unit test useTabReducer
userStories.description: As a developer, I want comprehensive tests for every tab reducer action so regressions like ID collisions and stale active-tab references are caught automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: New file tests/unit/tab-reducer.test.tsx with tests for every action type
Test: CREATE_TAB generates incrementing unique IDs
Test: CREATE_TAB after RESTORE_SESSION with tabs [tab-5, tab-3] produces tab-6 (not tab-1)
Test: CLOSE_TAB on the active tab selects the next tab (or previous if closing the last one)
Test: CLOSE_TAB on a non-active tab does not change activeTabId
Test: CLOSE_TAB on the only remaining tab sets activeTabId to null
Test: SELECT_TAB with a valid ID updates activeTabId
Test: SELECT_TAB with an ID not in tabs is a no-op
Test: UPDATE_CONTENT updates the correct tab's content without affecting other tabs
Test: RESTORE_SESSION replaces all tabs and activeTabId atomically
Test: RENAME_TAB updates filename and filePath for the target tab
Tests use renderHook from @testing-library/react to test the hook
Typecheck passes
All tests pass
userStories.notes: Since the reducer is exported as a pure function, most tests can call tabReducer(state, action) directly without renderHook. Use renderHook only for 1-2 tests that verify the hook integration. Reset the module-level ID counter between tests if needed (export a _resetIdCounter() test helper).

userStories.title: Extract useSessionPersistence hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I want session save/restore logic in its own hook so it can be tested independently and doesn't clutter the tab management code.
acceptanceCriteria: New file src/renderer/editor/useSessionPersistence.ts exports useSessionPersistence() hook
Hook accepts { tabs, activeTabId } as reactive inputs for save triggering
Hook returns { sessionReady: boolean, tabCursors: Record<string, CursorSnapshot>, updateCursor: (tabId, line, column) => void, onRestore: callback }
On mount, the hook calls window.matslop.sessionGetRestoreEnabled() and conditionally window.matslop.sessionGet()
Restored session data is passed through the onRestore callback so the parent can dispatch RESTORE_SESSION
When tabs or activeTabId change (after sessionReady), calls window.matslop.sessionSet() after a 400ms debounce
Registers a beforeunload listener that flushes session immediately
Cleans up debounce timer and beforeunload listener on unmount
Uses existing tabsToSession and sessionToTabs pure functions from sessionState.ts
Typecheck passes
All existing session-state unit tests pass
userStories.notes: The hook should accept an onRestore callback parameter (or use a ref to avoid re-triggering effects). The cursor tracking (tabCursors ref, updateCursor function) moves into this hook. The debounce timer ref and sessionReady ref are internal to the hook.

userStories.title: Unit test useSessionPersistence hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests verifying session restore, debounced save, and flush-on-unload behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: New file tests/unit/session-persistence-hook.test.tsx
Test: on mount with restore enabled, calls sessionGet() and invokes the restore callback with parsed tabs
Test: on mount with restore disabled, does not call sessionGet()
Test: on mount with corrupt session data, does not crash and invokes callback with null
Test: when tabs change, calls sessionSet() after 400ms debounce (use vi.advanceTimersByTime)
Test: rapid tab changes only trigger one sessionSet() call (debounce coalescing)
Test: beforeunload event triggers immediate sessionSet() call
IPC calls (window.matslop.*) are mocked via vi.fn() on a global window.matslop mock object
Typecheck passes
All tests pass
userStories.notes: Use vi.useFakeTimers() for debounce testing. Mock window.matslop as a plain object with vi.fn() methods. Use renderHook to test the hook lifecycle.

userStories.title: Extract useFileOperations hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I want file open/save/save-as logic in its own hook so EditorPanel doesn't mix file I/O with tab state management.
acceptanceCriteria: New file src/renderer/editor/useFileOperations.ts exports useFileOperations() hook
Hook accepts a dispatch function (from useTabReducer) and a tabs ref or getter for checking existing tabs
Hook exposes: openFile(), saveFile(tab), saveFileAs(tab), publishHtml(tab)

openFile() shows the file dialog via window.matslop.openFile(), checks if file is already open (dispatches SELECT_TAB if so), dispatches CREATE_TAB for new files, and calls window.matslop.recentFilesAdd()
saveFile(tab) saves to tab.filePath if it exists via window.matslop.saveFile(), otherwise calls saveFileAs
saveFile dispatches UPDATE_SAVED_CONTENT after a successful save
saveFileAs(tab) shows save dialog via window.matslop.saveFileAs(), dispatches RENAME_TAB + UPDATE_SAVED_CONTENT on success
.mls files are opened/created with mode 'livescript', .m files with mode 'script'
publishHtml(tab) generates HTML via the existing publishHtml() function and writes via window.matslop.publishWriteFile()
Typecheck passes
All existing tests pass
userStories.notes: The hook should accept a way to read current tabs (either a ref or a getTabs() callback) to check for already-open files. All IPC calls go through window.matslop.* — no new abstractions.

userStories.title: Unit test useFileOperations hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests for file open/save/save-as flows so regressions in file I/O are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: New file tests/unit/file-operations-hook.test.tsx
Test: openFile() with a new file dispatches CREATE_TAB with correct filename, content, filePath, and mode
Test: openFile() with an already-open file dispatches SELECT_TAB (no duplicate tab)
Test: openFile() when user cancels the dialog does nothing (no dispatch)
Test: saveFile() on a tab with filePath calls window.matslop.saveFile and dispatches UPDATE_SAVED_CONTENT
Test: saveFile() on an untitled tab (no filePath) delegates to saveFileAs
Test: saveFileAs() when user cancels does nothing
Test: saveFileAs() on success dispatches RENAME_TAB and UPDATE_SAVED_CONTENT
Test: .mls files are opened with mode 'livescript', .m files with mode 'script'
IPC calls mocked via vi.fn()
Typecheck passes
All tests pass
userStories.notes: Mock window.matslop.openFile to return { filename, content, filePath } or null. Mock dispatch as vi.fn() and verify it was called with the correct action objects.

userStories.title: Extract useScriptExecution hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I want script run/stop/section-run logic in its own hook so execution concerns are separated from tab management.
acceptanceCriteria: New file src/renderer/editor/useScriptExecution.ts exports useScriptExecution() hook
Hook accepts { getActiveTab, saveFile, engineStatus, onRun, onStop, onRunSection, getEditorInstance }
Hook exposes: run(), stop(), runSection(), runAndAdvance(), runWarning (string | null), clearRunWarning()
run() auto-saves the active tab before executing (calls saveFile), builds the command via buildRunScriptCommand(), and calls the onRun callback
run() for untitled tabs writes content to a temp file in the user's home directory via window.matslop.getHomeDir(), then runs from there
run() detects function-only files via isFunctionOnlyFile() and sets runWarning instead of running
runSection() uses findSectionRange() to extract the code at the cursor, then calls onRunSection
runAndAdvance() calls runSection() then moves the cursor via findNextSectionAdvanceLine()
stop() calls the onStop callback
Typecheck passes
All existing tests pass
userStories.notes: The hook needs access to the Monaco editor instance for cursor position (runSection) and cursor movement (runAndAdvance). Accept a getEditorInstance() getter. The runWarning state is managed inside the hook. clearRunWarning() is exposed so the parent can clear it on tab switch or typing.

userStories.title: Unit test useScriptExecution hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests for script execution flows including auto-save-before-run, function-only detection, and section execution.
acceptanceCriteria: New file tests/unit/script-execution-hook.test.tsx
Test: run() calls saveFile() before executing
Test: run() on a saved file builds command with buildRunScriptCommand() and calls onRun
Test: run() on an untitled file calls getHomeDir and builds a temp file run command
Test: run() on a function-only file sets runWarning and does NOT call onRun
Test: runSection() extracts code at cursor line using findSectionRange()
Test: stop() calls onStop
IPC and callbacks mocked
Typecheck passes
All tests pass
userStories.notes: Mock getActiveTab to return a tab object. Mock saveFile as vi.fn(). Mock the editor instance with getPosition() returning { lineNumber, column }. findSectionRange and buildRunScriptCommand are already tested in their own test files — just verify they are called with correct args.

userStories.title: Extract useEditorShortcuts hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I want keyboard shortcut registration in its own hook so the shortcut-to-action mapping is testable and EditorPanel doesn't handle key dispatch.
acceptanceCriteria: New file src/renderer/editor/useEditorShortcuts.ts exports useEditorShortcuts() hook
Hook accepts an action map Record<ShortcutAction, () => void> and registers/unregisters the ShortcutManager listener on mount/unmount
Hook loads custom shortcut overrides from window.matslop.configGetShortcuts() on mount
Hook applies overrides via applyShortcutOverrides() and updates the manager's active definitions
The ShortcutManager class itself is unchanged
Typecheck passes
All existing custom-shortcuts tests pass
userStories.notes: The ShortcutManager is a singleton class with setHandler(), setActiveDefinitions(), and destroy() methods. The hook should call setHandler(dispatch) on mount and setHandler(null) or similar on unmount. Load overrides once on mount, not on every render.

userStories.title: Unit test useEditorShortcuts hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests ensuring shortcuts register on mount, unregister on unmount, and dispatch the correct actions.
acceptanceCriteria: New file tests/unit/editor-shortcuts-hook.test.tsx
Test: mounting the hook registers a handler with ShortcutManager
Test: unmounting the hook clears the handler
Test: the handler dispatches the correct action from the action map when called
Test: custom shortcut overrides are loaded and applied on mount
Typecheck passes
All tests pass
userStories.notes: Mock the shortcutManager singleton. Verify setHandler and setActiveDefinitions are called. The ShortcutManager is imported from ../shortcuts/shortcutManager — mock that module.

userStories.title: Extract useDragDrop hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I want drag-and-drop file handling in its own hook so it doesn't add noise to the main editor component.
acceptanceCriteria: New file src/renderer/editor/useDragDrop.ts exports useDragDrop() hook
Hook accepts { onFilesDropped: (files: { path: string, name: string }[]) => void }
Hook returns { isDragOver: boolean, dragHandlers: { onDragEnter, onDragLeave, onDragOver, onDrop } }
onDragEnter/onDragLeave use a counter ref to handle nested drag events
onDrop filters for .m and .mls files and calls onFilesDropped with the list
onDragOver prevents default to allow drop
isDragOver is true only when a valid file drag is over the drop zone
Typecheck passes
All existing tests pass
userStories.notes: The existing EditorPanel uses dragCounterRef for the nested enter/leave counter pattern. Replicate that. The onDrop handler reads files from e.dataTransfer.files.

userStories.title: Unit test useDragDrop hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests for drag-drop file handling so edge cases are covered.
acceptanceCriteria: New file tests/unit/drag-drop-hook.test.tsx
Test: isDragOver becomes true on dragEnter and false on dragLeave
Test: nested dragEnter/dragLeave events don't flicker isDragOver (counter pattern)
Test: onDrop with .m files calls onFilesDropped with correct paths
Test: onDrop with non-.m/.mls files does not call onFilesDropped
Test: isDragOver resets to false after drop
Typecheck passes
All tests pass
userStories.notes: Create mock DragEvent objects with dataTransfer.files containing File objects. Use renderHook to test the hook and call the returned handlers.

userStories.title: Extract useMenuActions hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I want menu action dispatch in its own hook so menu-to-action wiring is isolated.
acceptanceCriteria: New file src/renderer/editor/useMenuActions.ts exports useMenuActions() hook
Hook accepts { menuAction, onMenuActionConsumed, actions } where actions maps menu action strings to handler functions
Hook processes menuAction in a useEffect, dispatches to the correct handler, and calls onMenuActionConsumed
Hook tracks the last processed menu action ID via ref to prevent duplicate processing
Menu actions handled: save, saveAs, newFile, newLiveScript, openFile, closeTab, run, runSection, runAndAdvance, publishHtml, and recentFile:* prefix
Typecheck passes
All existing tests pass
userStories.notes: The menuAction prop has shape { action: string, id: number } | null. The id field prevents duplicate processing — track the last processed id in a ref and skip if the same id is seen again.

userStories.title: Unit test useMenuActions hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests for menu action dispatch.
acceptanceCriteria: New file tests/unit/menu-actions-hook.test.tsx
Test: receiving a 'save' menu action calls the save handler
Test: receiving the same menu action ID twice only calls the handler once
Test: 'recentFile:/path/to/file.m' menu action calls the file-open handler with the path
Test: unknown menu action does not crash and calls onMenuActionConsumed
Typecheck passes
All tests pass
userStories.notes: Use renderHook and rerender with updated menuAction props to simulate menu action arrival.

userStories.title: Extract useDebugIntegration hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I want debugger-related editor behavior in its own hook.
acceptanceCriteria: New file src/renderer/editor/useDebugIntegration.ts exports useDebugIntegration() hook
Hook accepts { pausedLocation, tabs, activeTabId, dispatch, getEditorInstance, onFileSavedWhilePaused }
When pausedLocation changes to a file not currently open, the hook reads the file via window.matslop.readFile() and dispatches CREATE_TAB + SELECT_TAB
When pausedLocation changes to a file already open, the hook dispatches SELECT_TAB
After activating the paused tab, the hook moves the cursor to the paused line via editor.setPosition() and editor.revealLineInCenter()
The hook exposes isPaused: boolean derived from pausedLocation !== null
The hook exposes a notifyFileSaved(filePath) function that, when isPaused is true, calls onFileSavedWhilePaused and window.matslop.debugReapplyBreakpointsForFile(filePath)
Typecheck passes
All existing tests pass
userStories.notes: The existing EditorPanel has complex pausedLocation effect logic (~50 lines). The hook should replicate this behavior. The editor instance is needed for cursor positioning — accept a getEditorInstance() getter.

userStories.title: Unit test useDebugIntegration hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests for debugger integration.
acceptanceCriteria: New file tests/unit/debug-integration-hook.test.tsx
Test: when pausedLocation points to an open file, dispatches SELECT_TAB
Test: when pausedLocation points to a file not in tabs, reads the file and dispatches CREATE_TAB + SELECT_TAB
Test: when pausedLocation is null, isPaused is false
Test: notifyFileSaved() while paused calls onFileSavedWhilePaused with the file path
Test: notifyFileSaved() while paused calls debugReapplyBreakpointsForFile
IPC calls mocked
Typecheck passes
All tests pass
userStories.notes: Mock window.matslop.readFile to return file content. Mock dispatch as vi.fn(). Use renderHook and rerender with updated pausedLocation props.

userStories.title: Remove Welcome tab feature entirely
userStories.description: As a user, I don't want to see a Welcome tab — the 'No files open' empty state with New File / Open File buttons is sufficient.
acceptanceCriteria: WelcomeTab.tsx is deleted
All welcome-related CSS (.welcome-*) is removed from styles.css
The onCloseWelcome prop is removed from TabbedEditor
The 'welcome' mode is removed from EditorTabMode type in editorTypes.ts
The welcomeTabId state, welcomeTabIdRef, and welcomeInitRef are removed from EditorPanel
The configGetShowWelcome() and configSetShowWelcome() calls are removed
The WelcomeTab import is removed from TabbedEditor.tsx
The conditional rendering for welcome mode is removed from TabbedEditor.tsx
grep -r 'welcomeTab' src/ returns zero matches (case insensitive)
grep -r 'WelcomeTab' src/ returns zero matches
grep -r 'welcome' src/renderer/ returns zero matches except for any unrelated usage
Typecheck passes
All existing unit tests pass
userStories.notes: The session state serializer already filters out welcome tabs (sessionState.ts line 51). The welcome mode in EditorTabMode is 'script' | 'livescript' | 'welcome' — change to 'script' | 'livescript'. Check if any other code references the welcome mode and update accordingly. The startup code in EditorPanel should just leave tabs empty (the TabbedEditor empty state shows 'No files open').

userStories.title: Update E2E tests for Welcome tab removal
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all E2E tests updated to not depend on the Welcome tab.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/e2e/startup.spec.ts is updated — no longer asserts Welcome tab exists
Any E2E test that clicks 'Get Started' or interacts with the Welcome tab is updated or removed
Any E2E test that filters out the Welcome tab when counting editor tabs is simplified
Search all E2E test files for 'welcome' or 'Welcome' references and update each one
All 40 E2E tests pass (run with npm run test:e2e or npx playwright test)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Some E2E tests may create a session to bypass the Welcome tab. With it removed, those workarounds can be simplified. The startup.spec.ts probably asserts 'exactly one Welcome tab' — change to assert the empty state or no tabs.

userStories.title: Rewrite EditorPanel to compose extracted hooks
userStories.description: As a developer, I want EditorPanel to be a thin composition layer that wires the extracted hooks together, with no business logic of its own.
acceptanceCriteria: EditorPanel.tsx uses useTabReducer() for all tab state
EditorPanel.tsx uses useSessionPersistence() for session save/restore
EditorPanel.tsx uses useFileOperations() for open/save/save-as
EditorPanel.tsx uses useScriptExecution() for run/stop/section
EditorPanel.tsx uses useEditorShortcuts() for keyboard shortcuts
EditorPanel.tsx uses useDragDrop() for drag-and-drop
EditorPanel.tsx uses useMenuActions() for menu dispatch
EditorPanel.tsx uses useDebugIntegration() for debugger features
EditorPanel is under 250 lines (composition + JSX only, the AppContext fallback props code can push it slightly over 200)
No useState calls remain in EditorPanel except for the editor instance ref
The JSX return structure is unchanged — EditorToolbar, panel-content div, TabbedEditor
All existing E2E tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the big integration story. All hooks have been written and tested individually by this point. The AppContext prop-vs-context fallback pattern (lines 94-106 of current EditorPanel) should remain in EditorPanel — it's the composition layer's job to resolve prop sources. Wire the hooks together: useTabReducer provides state+dispatch, useSessionPersistence takes state and dispatches RESTORE_SESSION, useFileOperations takes dispatch, useScriptExecution takes getActiveTab from state, etc.

userStories.title: Remove TabbedEditor welcome mode rendering
userStories.description: As a developer, I want TabbedEditor to not have any welcome-mode conditional rendering since the Welcome tab is deleted.
acceptanceCriteria: TabbedEditor no longer imports WelcomeTab
The conditional rendering branch for activeTab.mode === 'welcome' is removed
The onCloseWelcome prop is removed from TabbedEditorProps interface
TabbedEditor renders only two content modes: livescript (LiveScriptEditor) and script (Monaco Editor)
Typecheck passes
All existing E2E tests pass
userStories.notes: This is a simple cleanup of TabbedEditor.tsx. The conditional rendering at line ~571 has three branches: welcome, livescript, script. Remove the welcome branch. The onCloseWelcome prop and its usage should already be gone from EditorPanel after US-R18/R20, but verify TabbedEditor itself is clean.

userStories.title: Extract editor CSS into scoped files
userStories.description: As a developer, I want editor CSS in its own files so changes to editor styling don't risk regressions in unrelated panels.
acceptanceCriteria: New file src/renderer/editor/editor.css contains all .editor-*, .tabbed-*, .toolbar-btn, .toolbar-separator, .toolbar-split-btn, .toolbar-dropdown, .toolbar-dropdown-item, .matslop-breakpoint-*, .matslop-paused-*, .matslop-section-*, .editor-run-warning* rules extracted from styles.css
New file src/renderer/editor/livescript.css contains all .ls-* rules extracted from styles.css
styles.css no longer contains any of the above rules
editor.css is imported by TabbedEditor.tsx (or EditorPanel.tsx)
livescript.css is imported by LiveScriptEditor.tsx
The .drop-overlay CSS stays in styles.css (it's used beyond the editor)
The .editor-panel and .editor-panel-content rules stay in styles.css (they're panel-level layout)
Visual appearance is unchanged in both dark and light themes
Typecheck passes
All existing tests pass
userStories.notes: This is a pure CSS extraction — cut rules from styles.css and paste into new files, then add import statements. Do NOT modify any CSS property values. Use exact copy-paste. The editor-toolbar CSS (.editor-toolbar class) should go into editor.css. The .toolbar-split-btn and .toolbar-dropdown rules (added earlier in this session) should also go into editor.css.

userStories.title: Remove stale code and dead references
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all code made dead by this refactor to be cleaned up.
acceptanceCriteria: syncNextTabId() in editorTypes.ts is removed (the reducer handles ID sync internally)
The global nextId counter in editorTypes.ts is removed (moved into useTabReducer.ts)

createTab() in editorTypes.ts is removed or updated — if the reducer generates IDs internally, createTab is no longer needed as a standalone export. If other code still calls createTab(), keep it but have it delegate to a shared ID generator.
No unused imports remain in any modified file (run lint to verify)
grep -r 'syncNextTabId' src/ returns zero matches
grep -r 'welcomeInitRef' src/ returns zero matches
grep -r 'welcomeTabIdRef' src/ returns zero matches
Typecheck passes
Lint passes (npm run lint) with zero errors in modified files
userStories.notes: Check if createTab() is used outside of EditorPanel (e.g., in tests or other components). If so, keep it but update it to use the shared ID generator from useTabReducer.ts. If it's only used in EditorPanel, remove it and have the reducer's CREATE_TAB action create tabs internally.

userStories.title: Component test for EditorPanel composition

Uses @testing-library/react with render()
Mocks all window.matslop.* IPC calls

Test: on mount with no session, renders 'No files open' empty state

Mocking Monaco: vi.mock('@monaco-editor/react', () => ({ default: (props) =>
{props.value}

Uses @testing-library/react with render()
Mocks Monaco editor component
Test: renders tab names in the tab bar

userStories.title: Component test for EditorToolbar
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a component test for the toolbar so button states and dropdown behavior are verified.
acceptanceCriteria: New file tests/unit/editor-toolbar.test.tsx
Uses @testing-library/react with render()
Test: 'New File' icon button calls onNewFile on click
Test: dropdown chevron toggles the new-file dropdown menu visibility
Test: clicking 'Live Script (.mls)' in dropdown calls onNewLiveScript

userStories.notes: Mock useOctaveStatus from OctaveContext to return 'ready'. The toolbar uses lucide-react icons — they render as SVG elements. Find buttons by title attribute.
